sections.paragraph:
fut. יִטֹּושׁ to send away, to let go (laffen).-

(1) to leave, i.e. to forsake, to desert (verlaffen), i.q. עָזַב; e.g. used of God as to a people, Jud. 6:13 1Sa 12:22 1Ki 8:57 2Ki 21:14 and on the other hand used of a people as to God, Deu 32:15 also to leave a thing, i.e. to let go (fahren laffen, aufgeben), 1Sa 10:2.

(2) to let go, i.q. to disperse, to spread abroad; compare נִשְׁמַט; 1Sa 30:16, נְטֻשִׁים “spread abroad;” Intrans. to spread oneself. 1Sa 4:2, וַתִּטּשׁ הַמִּלְחָמָה “and the battle spread itself;” compare Niph. No. 1.

(3) to commit to the care of any one; followed by עַל 1Sa 17:20, 22 1Sa 17:22, 28 1Sa 17:28.

(4) to let alone (liegen laffen), e.g. a field in the sabbatical year, Exo 23:11 to let rest (used of strife), Pro 17:14.

(5) to remit a debt, Neh 10:32.

(6) Followed by an acc. of pers. and לְ of thing, to admit some one to something, to permit it to him, Gen 31:28.

(7) to let go, and more strongly to cast out. Eze. 29:5 וּנְטַשְׁתִּיךָ הַמִּדְבָּרָה “I will cast thee out into a desert country,” Eze 32:4.

(8) to let out, i.e. to draw out (a sword), Isa 21:15 compare שָׁמַט. [In Thes. the idea given, as the primary meaning of this word, is that of striking; hence breaking, and thus casting off, letting go. The passage 1Sa 4:2, is referred to the idea of striking].

Niphal-

(1) to spread self abroad, used of the tendrils of the vine, Isa 16:8 of an army, Jud. 15:9 2Sa 5:18, 22 2Sa 5:22compare Kal No. 2.

(2) to be let go, i.e. loosened (as a rope), Isa 33:23.

(3) to be cast down, Amo 5:2 comp. Kal No. 7.

Pual, to be forsaken, Isa 32:14.

Derivative, נְטִישֹׁות.
